ops.text: Correction to the 2026-07-14T14:10 codex-wrapper papercut: the "surviving orphan" codex PID observed after the workflow completed was an UNRELATED session's codex run (its log greps Go files in internal/cli — the ccx repo), not the W1 implementation. The wrapper's backgrounded codex task died with the wrapper agent; the W1 work order never executed anywhere (workspace clean after the process exited). The mitigation stands and gains a step: wrapper prompts must force foreground codex exec, and before trusting ANY implementation lane's completion, the orchestrator verifies the target working copy actually changed — a matching-looking codex PID is not evidence the lane ran.
